title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Inference Brief: State Transition Contracts
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts state transition contracts increment in an exhaustive asynchronous state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Constrain legal state transitions and outputs without permitting impossible intermediate combinations.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
